$description: Single source of truth for cookie/privacy framework rules. Used by backend (Java) as ruleset engine and by frontend (UI) as framework selector. Extend by adding new entries to the 'frameworks' array following the same structure.

authority.name: European Data Protection Board (EDPB) + National DPAs

blocking.note: All non-essential cookies/trackers must be blocked until explicit consent. Includes scripts, pixels, local storage, session storage.

do_not_sell.note: GDPR does not have a 'Do Not Sell' concept. Uses consent/opt-in model instead.

signals.google_consent_mode_v2_note: Mandatory for EU/EEA/UK advertisers using Google products since March 2024

children.note: Member states can lower to 13. Verify per country.

cross_border.note: Data transfers outside EU/EEA require adequacy decision, SCCs, or BCRs.

frameworks.name: ePrivacy Directive
frameworks.short_description: EU cookie-specific directive — the original 'cookie law'

authority.name: Transposed into national law by each member state

blocking.note: Covers ALL storage/access on user devices, not just personal data. Broader than GDPR in this regard.

penalties.note: Penalties defined in national implementations. GDPR penalties may also apply for personal data aspects.

frameworks.name: TDDDG / TTDSG
frameworks.short_description: Germany's ePrivacy implementation — covers all device storage

authority.name: BfDI (Federal Commissioner for Data Protection) + Federal Network Agency

consent.bundled_consent_note: Bundled TTDSG+GDPR consent allowed if transparent about what user agrees to

blocking.note: Section 25 TDDDG: ALL storage/access on end-user devices requires consent. Broader than GDPR. Includes cookies, local storage, pixels, AND cookieless tracking. Also covers apps, smart home, IoT.

children.note: Under Art. 8 GDPR applied via TDDDG, minors under 18 need parental consent in Germany.

penalties.max_fine_note: TDDDG-specific. GDPR fines (up to €20M/4%) may also apply for personal data violations.

consent_management_ordinance.note: New German ordinance allows recognized consent management services to store preferences centrally, reducing repeated banners across sites.

frameworks.name: CCPA / CPRA
frameworks.full_name: California Consumer Privacy Act / California Privacy Rights Act
frameworks.short_description: California's flagship privacy law — opt-out model

authority.name: California Privacy Protection Agency (CPPA) + Attorney General

applies_if.note: Must meet at least ONE threshold

consent.note: Cookies can fire immediately. User must be able to opt out of sale/sharing. Opt-in only for minors.

blocking.note: No pre-consent blocking required. Scripts can run by default. But opt-out must genuinely stop sale/sharing when exercised.

banner.do_not_sell_link_text: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
banner.limit_sensitive_link_text: Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information
banner.note: Cookie banner not technically required. But 'Do Not Sell/Share' and 'Limit Sensitive' links ARE required. Banner is best practice.

do_not_sell.link_text: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information
do_not_sell.note: CPRA expanded 'sale' to include 'sharing' for cross-context behavioral advertising.

signals.note: GPC is MANDATORY. Must be honored as valid opt-out of sale/sharing.

logging.note: Not explicitly required by name, but must prove compliance with opt-out requirements.

frameworks.name: VCDPA
frameworks.short_description: Virginia's privacy law — opt-out with opt-in exceptions

consent.note: Opt-out default for general data. Opt-in required for sensitive data, children, and repurposed data.

blocking.note: No general blocking required. But if processing sensitive data via cookies, opt-in consent must be obtained first.

banner.note: No banner required for general processing. But must provide clear opt-out mechanism for targeted ads, data sales, and profiling.

do_not_sell.note: Broader opt-out scope than CCPA — covers targeted advertising, sale, AND profiling.

frameworks.name: LGPD
frameworks.full_name: Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados Pessoais

authority.name: ANPD (Autoridade Nacional de Proteção de Dados)

consent.note: Generic consent for all cookies is void. Must be granular by purpose. 'By using this site you accept cookies' is NOT valid.

blocking.note: Non-essential cookies should be blocked until opt-in consent.

layer_2.content: Detailed cookie info, purposes, retention periods, third parties, blocking options

banner.note: ANPD recommends two-layer approach. Must identify EACH third party by name (not just 'analytics').

logging.note: Controller must maintain adequate records and documentation of consent.

consent.note: Consent must be voluntary, specific, informed. Cookie walls not permissible. Both individuals AND juristic persons protected.

blocking.note: Trending toward GDPR-like approach. Interpretation still evolving.

banner.note: Banner should appear at user-friendly intervals (not every visit). Add Cookie Preferences in footer.

penalties.note: R1M-R10M fine AND/OR up to 10 years imprisonment.

frameworks.name: UK GDPR + PECR
frameworks.full_name: UK General Data Protection Regulation + Privacy and Electronic Communications Regulations
frameworks.short_description: UK's post-Brexit GDPR equivalent plus cookie regulations

consent.low_risk_exemptions_note: Data Use and Access Act (June 2025) introduced 5 narrow exemptions for low-risk cookies. Core analytics and advertising still require consent.
consent.note: Substantially identical to EU GDPR. ICO reviewed top 1,000 websites. 5 narrow low-risk exemptions added June 2025.

blocking.note: Core analytics and advertising cookies still require prior consent despite new exemptions.

frameworks.name: PIPEDA
frameworks.full_name: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

authority.name: Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC)

consent.note: Implied consent for low-risk, well-explained purposes. Express consent for sensitive or unexpected data use.

blocking.note: Implied consent may suffice for low-risk cookies. Express consent needed for sensitive/unexpected tracking.

penalties.note: Fines relatively low compared to GDPR.

frameworks.name: Quebec Law 25
frameworks.full_name: An Act to modernize legislative provisions as regards the protection of personal information

consent.note: Mandatory cookie consent banners. GDPR-like opt-in requirements.
Separate, explicit consent for tracking and profiling. Consent must be renewed for significant processing changes.

frameworks.name: APPI
frameworks.full_name: Act on the Protection of Personal Information

consent.note: Opt-in required specifically for third-party data transfers (relevant for advertising cookies sharing data with ad networks).

frameworks.name: DPDPA
frameworks.short_description: India's new comprehensive data protection law

consent.note: Must work in 22+ official Indian languages. One-click withdrawal mandatory.

consent_manager_registration.note: Only India-incorporated entities with ₹2 crore+ net worth can register as Consent Managers. Foreign CMPs excluded.

consent.deemed_consent_note: Deemed consent when user voluntarily provides data for reasonable purpose

blocking.note: No specific cookie law. PDPA applies when cookies collect personal data.

frameworks.name: UAE PDPL
frameworks.full_name: Federal Decree-Law No. 45 of 2021 (Personal Data Protection Law)

penalties.note: Executive regulations pending for detailed penalty framework.

consent.note: Currently notice-based with opt-out. Reform expected for closer GDPR alignment.

blocking.note: Currently no pre-consent blocking requirement. Reform may change this.

penalties.max_fine_alternative: 3x benefit obtained or 30% of adjusted turnover

_us_state_template._note: Most US state laws follow a similar pattern. Use this template to quickly add new states. Override fields as needed.

us_state_laws.name: TDPSA
us_state_laws.full_name: Texas Data Privacy and Security Act

overrides.note: Most business-friendly. No right to correct/delete third-party data.

us_state_laws.name: RIDTPPA
us_state_laws.full_name: Rhode Island Data Transparency and Privacy Protection Act

_extensibility_guide.how_to_add_new_framework: Copy any entry in 'frameworks' array. Fill in all fields. For US states, add to 'us_state_laws' array using '_us_state_template' as base and only specify 'overrides' for fields that differ.
_extensibility_guide.how_to_add_new_field: Add the field to the relevant object. Update '_enums' if adding a new enum type. All consumers of this JSON should handle unknown fields gracefully.
_extensibility_guide.versioning: Increment $version on every change. Use semver: major.minor.patch. Major = breaking schema change, Minor = new frameworks/fields, Patch = data corrections.
_extensibility_guide.java_usage: Deserialize into a PrivacyFramework POJO. Use 'consent.model' + 'blocking.strategy' + 'do_not_sell.required' as primary rule switches. Geo-target by matching user IP country/state to 'countries'/'states' fields.
_extensibility_guide.ui_usage: Use 'id' as select value, 'name' as display label, 'short_description' as tooltip. Filter by 'region' for grouped selectors. Use 'status' to show active/inactive badge.
